meta.title: Army Career Decision Tree — 11X Infantry / 18X Special Forces → 20-Year Retirement
meta.note: Two entry paths: 11X (infantry contract, no SOF guarantee) and 18X (direct-to-SF-selection guarantee). Each has a distinct risk/reward profile. Both can lead to the same endpoints — Ranger Regiment, Green Beret, Delta — but via different routes and timelines.

nodes.title: Pre-Enlistment / MEPS
nodes.description: ASVAB test (aim GT 110+). MEPS physical. Choose contract. Two primary paths: 11X (Infantry, with optional Option 40 Ranger addendum) or 18X (Special Forces Candidate — direct SFAS shot). Marriage before ship date = BAH with dependents from Day 1.

11X.risk: Lower risk — if Ranger/SF fails, you remain 11B in a regular infantry unit. You KNOW your MOS.
11X.reward: Proven path. Fleet infantry builds solid tactical foundation. Ranger and SF schools accessible. 11B is the best base for all SOF paths.
11X.option40_addendum: Add Option 40 to 11X contract for guaranteed RASP slot after OSUT + Airborne. Only ~8% of Option 40 contracts result in a Ranger Battalion assignment.

18X.risk: HIGH RISK — If you fail SFAS or Q Course, Army assigns MOS by needs of the Army (could become 92G food specialist, 88M truck driver, 42A HR, 92R parachute rigger). You have NO guaranteed infantry assignment.
18X.reward: Fastest legal path to Green Beret for civilians. Direct to SFAS after OSUT+Airborne+SFPC. Cuts 2-4 years off vs in-service path.
18X.pass_rate: Only ~20-30% who start the 18X pipeline become Green Berets. Roughly 8-20% of those who start OSUT as 18X graduate the Q Course.

options.label: YES
effects: E-1 Fort Moore / Fort Liberty w/dep BAH ~$1,500-2,000/mo depending on duty station
Does NOT allow family on post during OSUT/training

nodes.title: Infantry OSUT (11X) — One Station Unit Training
nodes.description: BCT + AIT combined into 22-week OSUT. Same company, same cadre throughout. Assigns 11B (Infantryman) or 11C (Indirect Fire Infantryman — 85% go 11B). If Option 40 contract attached: guaranteed RASP seat follows Airborne School.

phases.name: BCT Phase
phases.content: Weapons, land nav, physical conditioning, ACFT prep, values/discipline

phases.name: AIT Phase
phases.content: Infantry tactics, small unit ops, crew-served weapons, night ops, M4 expert qual

options.label: YES — proceed to Airborne School then RASP

nodes.title: Infantry OSUT (18X) — One Station Unit Training
nodes.description: Same 22-week OSUT as 11X. After graduation, 18X contracts proceed directly to Airborne School and then SFPC before SFAS — no fleet assignment first.

nodes.title: Basic Airborne Course (BAC) — Jump School

phases.name: Ground Week
phases.content: PLF drills, mock door, 34-ft tower, physical assessment

phases.name: Tower Week
phases.content: SLT qual, 250-ft tower, mass exit drills

phases.name: Jump Week
phases.content: 5 qualifying jumps from C-130/C-17 at 1,250 ft. Night jump required.

nodes.prerequisite_fitness: 60 pts per APFT event (17-21 age standard regardless of actual age). Age max 36.

nodes.title: Special Forces Preparation Course (SFPC)
nodes.description: Pre-selection conditioning course for 18X candidates. Physical standards ramp-up. Land navigation. Ranger history and standards. Swim assessment. Nutrition and recovery focus.

decisions.question: Complete SFPC and meet SFAS minimums?

options.label: NO — dropped for standards or injury

nodes.title: Special Forces Assessment and Selection (SFAS)
nodes.note_2026: Course extended from 24 to 35 days in 2026. Team Week now 8 days. Land nav increased 72-96 hrs. Unknown run/ruck events up to 40% longer. Weekly peer evaluations — lowest-rated candidate cut each week.
nodes.pass_rate: ~20-30% of all entrants receive Q Course seats
nodes.frequency: 4 cycles per year, ~300 candidates per class
requirements: Min APFT 206 pts (no event < 60 pts, age 17-21 standard)
E-4 to E-7 (enlisted). 18X may enter as E-2/E-3 under contract provision.
key_events: Gate Week — graded ruck marches, increasing weight/distance unknown
Land Navigation — solo, cross-country, map + compass, 72-96 hrs
Team Week (8 days) — small team SF-task missions
Peer evaluations — weekly, lowest peer-rated cut

nodes.title: SFAS Not Selected / Q Course Failure — 18X Risk
nodes.description: This is the core risk of the 18X contract. Army assigns MOS by needs of the Army. Cannot guarantee 11B. Most common involuntary reclasses: 92G (food), 42A (HR), 88M (truck driver), 92R (parachute rigger), 91F (small arms repair).

options.label: Involuntary reclass to support MOS — serve out contract
Some slip through to 82nd Airborne as 11B (rare — not guaranteed)
Request PSYOP/CA packet if selected — different SOF path
Wait for re-screen opportunity next SFAS cycle (not guaranteed)

nodes.title: SFQC Phase I — Individual Skills
nodes.content: In-processing at JFK Special Warfare Center. Small Unit Tactics, SF Tactics, Survival Skills, Language and Cultural intro training.

options.label: FAIL — returned to unit or reclassed

nodes.title: SFQC Phase II — MOS Qualification

mos_tracks.title: Weapons Sergeant
mos_tracks.content: All infantry and crew-served weapons systems, anti-armor, field-expedient weapons, foreign weapons. Direct action specialist.

mos_tracks.title: Engineer Sergeant
mos_tracks.content: Demolitions, construction, breaching, IED defeat, bridge building, route clearance.

mos_tracks.title: Medical Sergeant
mos_tracks.content: LONGEST track. Physician Assistant-level training. Combat trauma, surgery assist, dental, veterinary. Most respected MOS in SF. Nearly 1 year of medical school equivalent.

mos_tracks.title: Communications Sergeant
mos_tracks.content: All comms systems (SATCOM, HF, VHF), crypto, PACE planning. Most in-demand civilian skill bridge.

mos_tracks.title: Intelligence Sergeant
mos_tracks.content: Intel gathering, analysis, targeting. Available only after holding another 18-series MOS.

nodes.assignment_basis: GT score, DLAB language score, aptitude tests, personal preference, needs of SF

nodes.title: SFQC Phase III — Language Training
nodes.duration_weeks: 16-24 depending on DLAB score and language
nodes.content: Defense Language Institute or JFK SWC language training. Language assigned by SFAB regional alignment and DLAB score. Higher DLAB = more language options. Lower DLAB = more common languages.

nodes.title: SFQC Phase IV — Robin Sage (Collective Training)
nodes.location: Multiple counties in North Carolina — realistic UW environment
nodes.content: Culminating Unconventional Warfare exercise. Live civilian role players. 12-man ODA operates behind 'enemy lines.' Guerrilla force link-up, train/advise/assist, direct action missions. Failure here means no green beret.

options.label: Pursue Delta Force (CAG) selection — E5-E8, 4+ yrs SF experience
Stay in SF — become 18Z Team Sergeant, ODA command toward 20 years
Attend Ranger School (not required but highly respected)
Army Sniper School — add 3rd skill (5 weeks)

nodes.title: Ranger Assessment and Selection Program 1 (RASP 1)
additional_requirements: MOS in the 75th Ranger Regiment table of organization

phases.content: Physical and psychological eval, road marches, land nav, combatives, medical first responder, Ranger history. 12-mile ruck march w/35lb pack. 15-meter swim in uniform.
Ranger tactical skills, Ranger-specific weapons, special operations tasks, direct action, small boat ops, team assessment. 5-mile run in 40 min. 12-mile road march.

nodes.note: Option 40 = guaranteed RASP 1 seat, not guaranteed graduation. Only 8% of all Option 40 contracts result in Ranger Battalion assignment.

options.label: YES — Tan Beret earned. Assigned to Ranger Battalion.
FAIL — reassigned to 11B regular infantry unit

nodes.title: 75th Ranger Regiment — Assigned to Battalion
nodes.description: High-tempo LSCO/SOF unit. Most deployed unit in US Army. Multiple deployments per year. Build: CQB, direct action, airborne, air assault, sniper, JTAC, medical. Earn Ranger tab by attending Ranger School.

options.label: Attend Ranger School — earn Ranger Tab (strongly encouraged)
Attend SFAS — Green Beret path from Ranger Regiment
Stay in Ranger Regiment — senior NCO path to 20 years
Delta Force (CAG) — elite operators come heavily from 75th
Army Sniper School (5 wks, adds major value)

nodes.title: US Army Ranger School (61 days)
nodes.location: Fort Moore GA (Benning Phase) → Dahlonega GA (Mountain Phase) → Eglin AFB FL (Swamp/Jungle Phase)

phases.content: RAP week (physical screening), patrolling, small unit tactics, raids, ambushes
Mountain environment. Patrols, rappelling, mountaineering, cold weather ops
Water operations, jungle patrolling, exhaustion under stress. Final graded patrols

nodes.note: Ranger School ≠ 75th Ranger Regiment. Ranger School = tab (leadership school open to all MOS). Ranger Regiment = scroll (elite unit, requires RASP). Most Rangers in the Regiment eventually earn the tab by attending Ranger School.

options.label: YES — Ranger Tab earned
options.next: Return to unit or next goal

options.label: NO — recycled or dropped
options.next: Return to unit — re-attempt Ranger School

nodes.title: US Army Sniper School
nodes.location: Fort Moore GA — Harmony Church area
nodes.content: Marksmanship 300m-1500m, stalking and concealment, range estimation, observation and surveillance, 2-man sniper team planning, ballistics.

options.label: YES — Sniper qualification earned
options.next: Return to unit with additional qualification

options.label: Apply to SFAS — in-service Green Beret path (E-4 to E-7 eligible)
Get Option 40 equivalent via RASP volunteer — if MOS in Regiment TO&E
Ranger School — leadership credential, opens SOF doors
Army Sniper School — adds tactical value
Reenlist, change MOS (lat-move) — analyst, intel, EOD, etc.

nodes.title: Delta Force / 1st SFOD-D — Selection and OTC
nodes.location: Fort Liberty NC (Appalachian Mountains for selection events)
requirements: Active duty Army (Reserve and Guard occasionally)
Passing SQT score in primary MOS

selection_structure.content: Physical baseline, swim test, land nav intro
Appalachian Mountain land nav. Solo ruck marches increasing weight/distance daily. No time limits announced. No feedback. The Long Walk final test: 40-mile ruck with 45-lb pack over rough terrain.
Extensive psych eval of those surviving selection events

otc.name: Operator Training Course
otc.content: Turns selected candidates into operators. Advanced CQB, vehicle assault, hostage rescue, language, intelligence, and UW skills. Failure here (non-integrity) still allows Q Course path.

options.label: NOT SELECTED — return to SF/Ranger/infantry unit
OTC failure (non-integrity) — Q Course path available

nodes.title: 1st SFOD-D (Delta Force) — Operator
nodes.description: Nation's top-tier direct action and counterterrorism unit. Global operations. Small troop deployments, no public acknowledgment. Additional language, medical, and technical quals pursued continuously.

options.label: Extend beyond 20 for rank and higher pension

nodes.title: Senior NCO — Special Forces (18Z Team Sergeant)

options.label: Apply to Delta or SF (most DELTA operators come from Ranger Bn)
Apply to SF/Delta while time remains

nodes.title: Involuntary Support MOS (18X Risk)
nodes.description: If 18X pipeline fails at SFAS or Q Course, Army may involuntarily reclass to 42A, 92G, 88M, 92R, 91F, or other needs-of-Army MOS. Serve out contract. Options: reenlist for different MOS via lat-move, re-apply to SFAS if eligible, EAS at contract end.

nodes.title: PSYOP / Civil Affairs — SOF Alternative
nodes.description: Separate pipeline. Requires assessment and selection (POAS/CAAS) but less physically extreme than SF. PSYOP Qualification Course or CA Qualification Course at Fort Liberty. Deployable SOF assets assigned to theater special operations commands.

nodes.title: Army Lateral MOS Move
nodes.description: Requires reenlistment for new MOS. Unit, Army G1, and gaining branch all approve. ASVAB line score for new MOS required.

common_targets.title: SF Warrant Officer (OCS route)
common_targets.notes: Commission as WO via OCS + SFAS + Q Course as officer

common_targets.title: Night Stalkers — 160th Special Operations Aviation Regiment
common_targets.notes: Assessment and Selection open to rated and non-rated soldiers; SOAFS + individual MOS qualification

nodes.title: 20-Year Military Retirement
nodes.rank_typical: E-7 SFC to E-8 MSG/1SG (Infantry/Ranger). E-7 SFC to E-9 SGM (SF/Delta).

pension.BRS_entered_after_2018: All soldiers who entered after 2018 are under BRS

nodes.gi_bill: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Ch33) usable 15 yrs post-separation.
nodes.second_career: Pension + civilian salary ($60-100k) + VA disability = $90-130k+ total annual income typical.

nodes.title: Early Pipeline Failure — Medical or Admin

options.label: Admin separation — VA disability if service-connected. GI Bill if honorable.

nodes.title: Serve Beyond 20 Years
nodes.description: Each additional year adds 2.0% (BRS) pension. E-9 SGM vs E-7 SFC pension gap = $500-1,000+/mo for life. Worth considering if on E-8/E-9 promotion track.

marriage_timing.before_boot_camp: BAH w/dependents Day 1 of active duty. Significant income difference at E-1/E-2. Spouse may not accompany to training installations during OSUT/selection pipelines.
marriage_timing.after_OSUT: First opportunity to live together off-post. BAH w/dep kicks in if not already.
marriage_timing.during_pipelines: BAH w/dep + FSA $250/mo when deployed. Spouse must budget for solo living during deployments — SOF units deploy frequently.

tuition_assistance.note: Use ArmyIgnitED portal. Approved before class start. No service obligation for enlisted. Start at first permanent duty station. 2 classes/semester realistic.

college.CLEP_DSST: Free at most Army education centers. Can earn 30+ credit hours toward a degree. Use before or alongside TA.
college.gi_bill_strategy: Save Post-9/11 GI Bill for post-EAS or retirement. While on active duty: use TA exclusively. GI Bill while in service wastes the housing allowance benefit.
online_schools_army_compatible: Troy University — heavy military presence at Army bases

financial_rules.predatory_cars: Car lots outside Fort Moore, Fort Liberty, Fort Campbell target new soldiers. Never finance at a dealership near a base. Credit union (USAA, PenFed) or cash only.
financial_rules.roth_tsp: Open Day 1. Minimum 5% contribution for full BRS match after 2 years.
financial_rules.bah_strategy: Rent below BAH rate, invest the difference every month.
financial_rules.va_loan: 0% down, no PMI. Use at 2+ year duty station. Buy a property, rent it at PCS, build equity.
financial_rules.srb_reenlistment_bonus: Invest immediately — TSP or Roth IRA ($7,000 limit 2026).
financial_rules.combat_zone_pay: All pay is federal income tax-free in designated combat zones. Max Roth IRA during deployment.
financial_rules.RANGER_DELTA_note: Special pays stack: jump ($150) + HALO ($225) + SODI/SDAP + hostile fire ($225) + FSA ($250) deployed. GySgt-equivalent with SOF pays can see $8,000-10,000+ all-in monthly.

18X_pros: Fastest civilian path to Green Beret
18X_cons: Fail = potentially any support MOS by needs of Army
18B through E medic training etc carry extreme washout risk
If 18D track: 50+ weeks of training after SFAS
11X_pros: Floor is always 11B infantry — a respected, employable, combat credential
Ranger (Option 40 addendum) to SF pipeline is battle-tested
Build tactical maturity before SFAS — statistically better SF candidates come from fleet
Can apply to SFAS as E-4 to E-7
11X_cons: Slower path to SF by 2-4 years
Option 40 only 8% conversion to Ranger Bn
18X_vs_11X_frank_comparison.verdict: For someone whose #1 goal is becoming a Green Beret and is willing to risk a support MOS, 18X is faster. For someone who wants to be infantry, serve in Ranger Regiment, THEN pursue SF/Delta, the 11X + Option 40 path produces a better overall career foundation and does NOT risk involuntary reclassification to a kitchen.
